creator: vermaden@interia.pl
text:
Whole x11/xorg locks the machine after usage of xrandr(1) command, to be more precise machine does not locks up 'just after' issuing the xrandr(1) command (without arguments), but after killing x11/xorg with 'three kings' or should I say with CTRL-ALT-BACKSPACE shortcut.

The console is now unusable, with some characters very fast 'flying' thru te screen (top part of it, like only the 1st top line), if I press POWER button here, FreeBSD will gracefully shutdown the system, no need for fsck(8) after reboot, etc. 

I have described/tested it with openbox/twm/awesome window managers, so I assume its not a WM issue but a xrandr/x11 issue.

Below I attach my /etc/X11/xorg.conf | dmesg | pciconf -l -v

Regerds,
vermaden

% cat /etc/X11/xorg.conf 
Section "ServerFlags"
  Option "DontZap"         "off"
  Option "AllowEmptyInput" "off"
  Option "AutoAddDevices"  "off"
  # Option "AIGLX"           "on"
  # Option "RANDR"           "on"
EndSection

Section "InputDevice"
  Identifier "Keyboard0"
  Driver     "kbd"
  Option     "XkbOptions" "terminate:ctrl_alt_bksp,ctrl:nocaps"
  Option     "XkbLayout" "pl"
EndSection

Section "ServerLayout"
  Identifier  "xorg0"
  Screen 0    "screen0"   0 0
  InputDevice "mouse0"    "CorePointer"
  InputDevice "keyboard0" "CoreKeyboard"
EndSection

Section "Module"
  Load "dbe"
  Load "extmod"
  Load "dri"
  Load "glx"
EndSection

Section "InputDevice"
  Identifier "mouse0"
  Driver     "mouse"
  Option     "Protocol"     "auto"
  Option     "Device"       "/dev/sysmouse"
  Option     "ZAxisMapping" "4 5 6 7"
EndSection

Section "Monitor"
  Identifier "monitor0"
  Option     "DPMS"
EndSection

Section "Device"
  Identifier "gfx0"
  Driver     "intel"
  Option     "DPMS"
EndSection

Section "Screen"
  Identifier "screen0"
  Device     "gfx0"
  Monitor    "monitor0"
  SubSection "Display"
    Depth 24
    Virtual 5000 2000
  EndSubSection
EndSection

Section "Files"
  ModulePath "/usr/local/lib/xorg/modules"
  FontPath   "/usr/local/lib/X11/fonts/misc/"
  FontPath   "/usr/local/lib/X11/fonts/TTF/"
  FontPath   "/usr/local/lib/X11/fonts/OTF"
  FontPath   "/usr/local/lib/X11/fonts/Type1/"
  FontPath   "/usr/local/lib/X11/fonts/100dpi/"
  FontPath   "/usr/local/lib/X11/fonts/75dpi/"
EndSection


----

How-To-Repeat: [install FreeBSD 8.1-RELEASE]
# pkg_add -r xorg openbox
# X -configure
# cp xorg.conf.new /etc/X11/xorg.conf (with some mine modifications)
# su - user
% cat > ~/.xinitrc << __EOF
xterm &
openbox
__EOF
% xinit
[after x11/xorg started enter xrandr(1) command into the xterm(1) (without argumrnts]
[now 'kill' x11/xorg with CTRL-ALT-BACKSPACE]

Sometimes its needed to start x11/xorg by xinit more then once to reproduce the BUG, but no moew then 3-4 times, for most of the time it happens on the first shot.
